name: Set
shortDescription: Set is the Egyptian god of chaos, storms, and the desert. He is best known for murdering his brother Osiris and challenging Horus for the throne.
description: Set, also known as Seth or Setekh, is a complex and often misunderstood figure in Egyptian mythology. He is the god of chaos, storms, the desert, and foreign lands. While often portrayed as a villain, particularly due to his role in the murder of his brother Osiris, Set was also a powerful and important deity who played a crucial role in the balance of the cosmos. He was associated with strength, protection, and sometimes even considered a protector of Ra, the sun god, during his nightly journey through the underworld, battling the serpent Apophis. Set's association with the desert reflects the Egyptian view of the desert as a place of danger and disorder, but also a source of valuable resources. His identification with foreign lands further emphasizes his role as an outsider and a force of disruption. The conflict between Set and Horus is a central theme in Egyptian mythology, representing the struggle between chaos and order, and ultimately, the triumph of divine kingship. The Eye of Horus is a powerful symbol associated with Horus's victory over Set and the restoration of order. Although he is often viewed negatively, Set was also worshiped in various regions of Egypt, particularly during the Second Dynasty. His character evolved over time, becoming increasingly demonized as the cult of Osiris gained prominence. Despite this negative portrayal, Set's significance in Egyptian mythology cannot be denied, as he represents the ever-present potential for chaos and disruption that exists alongside order and stability.

sources.title: Egyptian Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Goddesses, and Traditions of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Geraldine Pinch
